tls13_cipher_suites.label: TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
tls13_cipher_suites.notes: Recommended for high-security deployments. Quantum-vulnerable via Grover's algorithm but effective key length drops to 128-bit — still acceptable post-Q-day.

tls13_cipher_suites.label: TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
tls13_cipher_suites.notes: Widely deployed. Grover's algorithm reduces effective security to 64-bit — below NIST's post-quantum minimum. Avoid for data with long confidentiality requirements.

tls13_cipher_suites.label: TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256
tls13_cipher_suites.notes: Preferred on mobile/embedded (no AES hardware acceleration). Same quantum risk as AES-128.

key_exchange.label: X25519
key_exchange.notes: Dominant in TLS 1.3. Vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on a cryptographically relevant quantum computer.

key_exchange.label: P-256 (secp256r1)
key_exchange.notes: NIST P-256 ECDH. Widely deployed in enterprise TLS. Vulnerable to quantum attack.

key_exchange.label: ML-KEM-768
key_exchange.notes: NIST-standardised KEM (FIPS 203). Security Level 3 — roughly equivalent to AES-192 classically. Recommended for most PQC TLS deployments.

key_exchange.label: ML-KEM-1024
key_exchange.notes: NIST Security Level 5. Comparable to AES-256. Larger handshake overhead; use when maximum assurance is required.

key_exchange.label: X25519+ML-KEM-768 (Hybrid)
key_exchange.notes: Combines X25519 (classical) with ML-KEM-768 (PQC). Provides forward secrecy against both classical and quantum adversaries. IETF-recommended migration path. Chrome and Cloudflare default.

key_exchange.label: P-256+ML-KEM-768 (Hybrid)
key_exchange.notes: P-256 + ML-KEM-768 hybrid. Preferred in FIPS-constrained environments where P-256 is mandated.

signature_schemes.label: ECDSA P-256 + SHA-256
signature_schemes.notes: Most common TLS certificate type. Vulnerable to Shor's algorithm. Migrate to ML-DSA or SLH-DSA for post-quantum assurance.

signature_schemes.label: RSA-PSS 2048 + SHA-256
signature_schemes.notes: Legacy RSA. Quantum-vulnerable and below NIST 128-bit minimum even classically. Only use where legacy compatibility is required.

signature_schemes.label: ML-DSA-44
signature_schemes.notes: Smallest ML-DSA variant. Appropriate for TLS CertificateVerify where handshake size is critical. NIST Security Level 2.

signature_schemes.label: ML-DSA-65
signature_schemes.notes: Balanced ML-DSA variant. Security Level 3. Recommended for CA certificates and long-lived identities.

signature_schemes.label: ML-DSA-87
signature_schemes.notes: Highest-assurance ML-DSA variant. Security Level 5. Use for root CAs and critical infrastructure where maximum security is mandatory.

handshake_size_comparison.description: Approximate TLS 1.3 handshake payload sizes for key exchange and authentication
